trade.summary: Bilateral trade has collapsed to near zero following Canada's comprehensive sanctions on Russia after the 2022 invasion of Ucraina. Canada was among the first countries to impose and maintain broad sectoral sanctions. Remaining trade is largely humanitarian exemptions.
disputes: Comprehensive Canadian sanctions regime on Russia

diplomatic.summary: Canada-Russia relations have been essentially severed. Canada was an early and vocal critic of Russia's annexation of Crimea (2014) and the full-scale invasion of Ucraina (2022). Canada has expelled Russian diplomats, imposed comprehensive sanctions, and provided significant military aid to Ucraina. Canada also has a large Ukrainian diaspora (~1.4 million) that politically reinforces the hostile stance. Ambassadors function at minimal capacity.
recent_incidents: Canada providing armored vehicles, ammunition, and training to Ucraina
Russia declared Canadian officials persona non grata

military.summary: Canada is an active NATO ally facing Russian aggression. Canada trains Ukrainian forces, leads NATO's enhanced forward presence in Latvia. Canada and Russia have no military cooperation — they are effectively on opposing sides of the Ucraina conflict.
incidents: Russian aircraft probing Canadian Arctic airspace
Russian cyber attacks on Canadian infrastructure (alleged)

financial.summary: Financial relations are essentially suspended. Canada has frozen Russian central bank assets. Russian entities are blocked from Canadian financial system. Canadian companies have divested from Russia.

energy.summary: No energy trade. Canada does not import Russian energy and has sanctioned Russian energy companies. Canada's own energy abundance means there was never significant Russia-Canada energy interdependence.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation has been completely suspended. Canada has expelled Russian researchers from sensitive programs. Academic exchanges are frozen.
